REPLY TO DEFENDANTS OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFFS MOTION FOR LEAVE TO AMEND (TRANSACTION ID # 62391748) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MASSOL, ALEXIS
ORDER GRANTING PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF MASSOL'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THERE IS A LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENT AND DEFENDANTS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE PREJUDICE. THE DECLARATION OF COUNSEL BROPHY INDICATES THAT DEFENDANTS HAVE YET TO CONDUCT DISCOVERY. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION SUBSTANTIALLY COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1324 AND DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION TO THE MOTION ON THE MERITS WAIVES ANY SERVICE DEFECT. (SEE CARLTON V. QUINT (2000) 77 CAL.APP.4TH 690, 697.) GIVEN THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE PROPOSED AMENDMENTS, THE WISER COURSE OF CONDUCT IS TO PERMIT AMENDMENT AND ALLOW DEFENDANTS TO TEST THE SUFFICIENCY OF THE NEW CLAIMS OR ALLEGATIONS BY DEMURRER OR OTHER APPROPRIATE PROCEEDINGS. (SEE CAL. CASUALTY GEN. INS. CO. V. SUP. CRT. 173 CAL.APP.3D 274, 280-281.) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M.GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, TORREANOM@AOL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Plaintiff'S Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-26-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ALEXIS MASSOL's Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint.

Plaintiff Massol's motion for leave to file second amended complaint is GRANTED. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment and defendants fail to demonstrate prejudice. The declaration of counsel Brophy indicates that defendants have yet to conduct discovery. Plaintiff's motion substantially complies with CRC 3.1324 and defendant's opposition to the motion on the merits waives any service defect. (See Carlton v. Quint (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690, 697.) Given the issues raised by the proposed amendments, the wiser course of conduct is to permit amendment and allow defendants to test the sufficiency of the new claims or allegations by demurrer or other appropriate proceedings. (See Cal. Casualty Gen. Ins. Co. v. Sup. Crt. 173 Cal.App.3d 274, 280-281.)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M.Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)
